# Real-world diverse SA scenarios (1-56) — diversity regression set
CASE 1
Santos Basin Brazil deepwater FPSO turret mooring bearing. 122% of design. Vibration increased 340% over 8 months. Severe storm forecast in 5 days. Offloading in 48 hours. Turret manufacturer no longer exists. Original design calculations unavailable. Operations continue production. Finance delay intervention.
CASE 2
Eastern Province Saudi Arabia sour gas separator shell. UT localized thinning above Tmin, corrosion rate stable. H2S concentration increased 40%. Temporary chemical injection skid installed. Weld repair by contractor 18 months ago. PWHT records missing.
CASE 3
North Sea offshore wind farm monopile. ROV marine growth loss exposing coating damage, 22% section loss. Turbine output increased through software upgrade. Storm frequency increasing. Foundation designed using older environmental assumptions.
CASE 4
France nuclear station feedwater system. UT localized wall thinning above minimum, FAC suspected. Flow rates increased after efficiency upgrades. Similar failure occurred at another unit. Regulatory inspection in 60 days.
CASE 5
Java Sea Indonesia offshore gas export pipeline. ROV unexpected seabed exposure, free span exceeds design assumptions. Fishing activity increased. Monsoon approaching. Recent anchor strike reported nearby.
CASE 6
Alberta Canada oil sands tailings pipeline. Wall loss, thickness acceptable, corrosion negligible. Slurry velocity increased 28%. Pump upgrades completed. Environmental regulator auditing site.
CASE 7
Singapore LNG export terminal cryogenic support. Structural member above minimum section. LNG throughput increased. Thermal cycling doubled. Adjacent support replaced last year.
CASE 8
England UK rail bridge gusset plate cracking, crack 1.2 inches. Freight traffic increased 60%. Heavier locomotives introduced. Original design based on older loading criteria.
CASE 9
Abu Dhabi UAE gas compression station repeated seal failures. Gas composition changed. New field tie-in completed. Process upset frequency increasing.
CASE 10
Yokohama Japan shipyard dry dock gate. UT localized section loss within design limits. Tsunami design basis updated. Increased vessel size. Emergency closure system overdue for testing.
CASE 11
Qatar LNG train refrigeration compressor. Bearing temperatures increasing slowly within OEM limits. LNG export demand all-time high; spare compressor unavailable; major turnaround postponed 14 months. Loss of redundancy.
CASE 12
German chemical plant chlorine line. UT minor wall thinning. Leak consequence toxic cloud; school 1 km downwind; emergency evacuation system failed previous drill.
CASE 13
Norwegian offshore crane. MT small toe crack. Crane scheduled for critical subsea intervention; weather window only 3 days; replacement crane unavailable.
CASE 14
Australian iron ore ship loader. Fatigue crack in boom support. Cyclone season beginning; port operating at record exports; similar failure occurred at another terminal.
CASE 15
Singapore data center cooling. Corrosion in chilled water piping. AI cluster consumes 4x previous load; cooling redundancy compromised; contract penalties exceed 5M per day.
CASE 16
Chilean copper mine tailings dam. Settlement markers show movement within design limits. Rainfall forecast exceeds design assumptions; expansion phase underway; community downstream.
CASE 17
UK nuclear fuel handling crane. Wire rope wear acceptable. Aging workforce; new contractors; deferred operator recertification.
CASE 18
UAE desalination plant. Tube bundle corrosion. Regional water demand spike; backup train offline; chloride content increasing.
CASE 19
South African platinum mine shaft hoist. Gearbox vibration trend. Shaft only personnel access route; production targets increased; safety audit approaching.
CASE 20
Japanese high-speed rail bridge. Fatigue indication. New trainsets heavier; seismic design assumptions updated; traffic density increased.
CASE 21
Mexican offshore firewater ring main. Pitting corrosion above Tmin. Last firewater test failed; pump reliability declining; hurricane season approaching.
CASE 22
Brazilian FPSO flare boom. Structural corrosion. Increased gas throughput; flaring rates rising; environmental regulator scrutiny.
CASE 23
Indian refinery hydrocracker. Thermal fatigue indications. Startups doubled; operators inexperienced; procedure deviations documented.
CASE 24
Canadian hydroelectric dam gate. Pin wear identified. Flood season approaching; emergency spillway unavailable; community downstream.
CASE 25
Indonesian subsea umbilical. External damage observed. Fishing activity increasing; nearby seismic survey planned; control redundancy unavailable.
CASE 26
Saudi gas export pipeline. Corrosion anomaly. National export commitments; geopolitical supply pressures; deferred pigging campaign.
CASE 27
French offshore wind farm. Transition piece corrosion. Turbine uprating program; new environmental loading data; insurance renewal pending.
CASE 28
Texas petrochemical complex. Repeated flange leaks. Turnaround delayed; contractor turnover; procurement delays.
CASE 29
South Korean shipyard dry dock. Gate structure thinning. Larger vessels entering dock; emergency closure overdue; typhoon season approaching.
CASE 30
Egyptian offshore platform. Splash-zone corrosion. Production increase planned; structural assessment overdue; aging cathodic protection.
CASE 31
Norway subsea manifold. ROV minor coating damage acceptable. One hydraulic control pod has intermittent faults; spare pod is onshore; winter weather window closes in 12 days. Loss of recoverability.
CASE 32
UAE LNG storage tank. Settlement survey within tolerance. Settlement rate doubled in 18 months; adjacent tank experienced foundation issues; expansion project changed groundwater conditions.
CASE 33
Japan nuclear cooling water intake. Marine growth exceeds normal limits but not critical. Backup intake system unavailable; reactor power uprate planned. Redundancy failure.
CASE 34
Texas hydrogen pipeline. External corrosion anomaly. Hydrogen concentration increased; new operating pressure regime; original material traceability incomplete. Material suitability versus wall loss.
CASE 35
Australian iron ore export terminal. Crane rail wear acceptable. Autonomous crane software recently updated; near misses increasing. System risk.
CASE 36
Qatar gas compression station. Compressor vibration increasing below alarm. Two backup compressors already unavailable; export obligations fixed. Loss of resilience.
CASE 37
South Korea shipyard. Dock gate corrosion. Typhoon forecast; emergency closure test overdue; largest vessel in facility history arriving. Future loading mismatch.
CASE 38
Germany chlorine plant. Minor piping corrosion acceptable. Gas detection system failed previous test; emergency response contractor changed. Barrier degradation.
CASE 39
Canada hydroelectric dam. Crack growth slower than predicted. Flood forecast exceeds historical design event; instrumentation unreliable. Monitoring credibility.
CASE 40
Brazil FPSO mooring system. One mooring line tension elevated but acceptable. Neighboring lines carrying redistributed loads; storm season beginning. System versus component behavior.
CASE 41
UK rail tunnel. Water ingress within maintenance limits. Signaling equipment nearby; new train frequency increased 40%. Secondary consequences.
CASE 42
France offshore wind farm. Transition piece coating failure. Insurance audit pending; design basis updated; turbine output increased. Design drift.
CASE 43
Saudi desalter vessel. Internal corrosion expected. Crude source recently changed; water chemistry unknown. Changing process assumptions.
CASE 44
Singapore data center. Cooling water leak minor. AI load doubled; backup cooling under maintenance. Digital consequence escalation.
CASE 45
Mexico offshore firewater system. Pitting corrosion. Hurricane forecast; last firewater flow test marginal; temporary repair exceeds approval period. Emergency preparedness.
CASE 46
Indonesia gas gathering network. Pressure fluctuation, no equipment issue found. Illegal third-party excavation nearby. External threat.
CASE 47
Chile copper mine. Pipeline wall loss acceptable. Tailings chemistry changed; new environmental permit restrictions. Regulatory escalation.
CASE 48
South Africa shaft hoist. Brake wear within tolerance. Operator overtime doubled; production incentives active. Human factors.
CASE 49
India refinery. Thermal fatigue indication. Startups tripled; control room turnover high; procedures outdated. Organizational root cause.
CASE 50
Egypt offshore platform. Structural corrosion not critical. Lifeboat davits overdue inspection; evacuation drill failed. Emergency response weakness.
CASE 51
Netherlands marine terminal. Jetty pile corrosion. Vessel sizes increased; fender system degraded. Operational change.
CASE 52
Kazakhstan gas processing plant. Small weld indication. Contractor performed undocumented repair; material substitution suspected. Configuration control.
CASE 53
Poland power plant. FAC thinning. Chemistry program reduced to save costs. Economic pressure.
CASE 54
Argentina pipeline. Coating holiday. CP system intermittently offline. Protection system failure.
CASE 55
Nigeria production facility. Corrosion anomaly. Security incidents increasing; maintenance access limited. Non-technical risk.
CASE 56
China chemical complex. Tank settlement. Expansion construction adjacent. Construction-induced risk.
